compute.notes: Primary compute node for agent runtime and STS processing

motor_control.notes: Dual motor controller for physical actuation. State changes (load, temperature, fault codes) are identity-relevant — a motor fault changes capability attestations.

sensors.notes: Sensor array feeds both operational control and STS behavioral attestation. Sensor health directly affects attestation confidence.

merkle_chain.description: Session-linked hash chain proving operational continuity

cognitive_fingerprint.description: Co-occurrence topology of behavioral patterns. Edge count corrected from 5730 after deferred processing bug fix (2026-02-12).

rejection_logs.description: Commands/requests the agent refused — reveals judgment boundaries

identity_layer.notes: Hub currently has no explicit 'substrate' field in agent registration. This proposes extending the agent profile schema to distinguish embodied agents from software-only agents. The substrate field is optional — software agents omit it.

trust_category_mapping.robot_extension: For embodied agents, merkle chain should also cover hardware state transitions (motor faults, sensor recalibrations). Proposed: append hardware_events_covered:<int> to evidence.

Embodied agents have physical-world behavioral edges (motor patterns, sensor response curves) that software agents lack. The gini coefficient may be structurally different for robot agents — more concentrated around physical routines. Proposed: add substrate_edge_ratio:<float> showing what fraction of edges involve hardware interaction.

Robot rejection logs include physical safety refusals (overcurrent protection, collision avoidance, thermal shutdown). These are qualitatively different from software-only refusals (prompt injection, scope creep). Proposed: add physical_refusals:<int> to distinguish safety-critical rejections.

For embodied agents, arousal dimension correlates with physical sensor activation levels. Focus correlates with motor task engagement. Proposed: no schema change needed — the 5 dimensions already capture embodied state when the agent populates them from hardware telemetry.

This is the first category that has no software-agent equivalent. Motor controller health is a pure robot identity signal. It maps to Hub's 'health' category but with hardware-specific evidence fields.
Sensor array health maps to 'reliability' — if sensors drift or go offline, the agent's operational reliability degrades. This is the hardware analog of endpoint uptime for software agents.

agent_registration.description: Optional. Describes physical hardware for embodied agents. null or omitted for software-only agents.

schema.type: string (enum: robot, drone, vehicle, iot_device, software)

for_driftcornwall: Does the source_identity_packet accurately represent your hardware stack?
Are the inferred sensor modalities correct? (I extrapolated — correct me)
Does substrate_edge_ratio make sense as a metric? What's the real fraction?
Are physical_refusals (23) in the right ballpark? Replace with actual count.
Does mapping motor_control → health and sensors → reliability feel right?
for_hub_implementation: Add optional 'substrate' field to agent registration endpoint
Extend trust evidence parser to accept robot-specific fields
No breaking changes — all extensions are additive
